findings.summary: Shared utility abstraction has competing public entry points
evidence: src/shared/components/StyledVideoPlayer.tsx imports cn directly from @/shared/lib/uiClassNames instead of using the shared utils surface.
Within this reviewed slice, the same helper is reachable through multiple paths (utils and uiClassNames).
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical import surface for cn. Either: 1) remove the re-export from src/shared/lib/utils.ts and migrate callers to uiClassNames, or 2) keep utils.ts as the canonical facade and enforce it with an import restriction so direct uiClassNames imports are limited to lib internals.
findings.reasoning: Multiple entry points weaken abstraction boundaries and make future refactors more expensive because consumers are split across inconsistent interfaces.
